steps.title: Identify the core conflict
steps.content: The passage establishes one key fact: Karenin hates change because his sense of time is fundamentally cyclical. The opening (“Karenin hated change”) is immediately followed by an explanation of dog time as circular, moving “round and round the face...following the same path.” When disrupted (furniture moved), this pattern is disturbed.

steps.title: Match the governing principle to the choices
steps.content: Answer A captures his core nature: he has “a strong preference for predictability” (the need to maintain his cyclical time) and “an aversion to disruption” (why changes to his environment upset him). The other choices either misframe this as a recent worsening (B), falsely extend Karenin's view of time to Tomas and Tereza (C), or introduce acceleration the text doesn't mention (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
